rag.description: The CloseChildWindows action closes all child browser windows or a specified number of them, always preserving the main window at index zero and returning driver focus to it after completion. It collects all non-main window handles, reverses the list, and closes each one with a 100 ms delay between closures. Individual closure exceptions are captured and stored without aborting the sequence. When only one window handle exists the action returns immediately as a no-op. Use the optional integer Argument to limit closure to an exact count; omit it to close all children.

qa.question: What is the primary purpose of the CloseChildWindows action?
qa.answer: It closes all child browser windows or tabs while always preserving the main window at index zero and returning WebDriver focus to that main window after completion.

qa.question: What happens when no Argument is provided to CloseChildWindows?
qa.answer: All child windows beyond the main window at index zero are closed in reverse handle order, and WebDriver focus is returned to the main window.

qa.question: How does the Argument parameter control how many windows are closed?
qa.answer: Argument is parsed as an integer specifying the maximum number of child windows to close. Values exceeding the actual child count are silently clamped. A negative or zero value closes no windows. A non-numeric value is treated as absent, closing all children.

qa.question: How does CloseChildWindows handle an error when closing an individual window?
qa.answer: Each window closure is wrapped in a try-catch. If an exception occurs the exception is captured and stored in the plugin response and iteration continues to the next window without aborting the loop.

qa.question: What happens when only one browser window is open?
qa.answer: The action detects a single window handle and returns immediately without attempting any closures, making it a safe no-op in single-window sessions.

qa.question: What aliases can be used in place of CloseChildWindows?
qa.answer: The action can also be invoked using CloseAllChildWindows, CloseAllTabs, or CloseTabs.

qa.question: What W3C WebDriver endpoint does CloseChildWindows use?
qa.answer: CloseChildWindows calls the W3C WebDriver DELETE /session/{session id}/window endpoint for each child window closure.

qa.question: What is the manifest version for CloseChildWindows?

description: ### Purpose
Closes all child browser windows or tabs while always preserving the main window at index zero.
An optional integer Argument limits closure to an exact number of children; omitting it closes all of them.
After all targeted windows are closed the driver focus is returned to the main window, leaving the session in a consistent, single-window state.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                   | Description                                                                                                     |
| Selective Closure         | Optional Argument limits closure to an exact number of child windows; omit to close all children.               |
| Main Window Preservation  | The window at index zero is always retained and receives driver focus after all closures complete.              |
| Reverse-Order Processing  | Child handles are iterated in reverse order for consistent stack-based closing behavior.                        |
| Per-Window Error Capture  | Exceptions thrown during individual window closures are captured and stored without aborting the overall loop.  |
| Paced Execution           | A 100 ms delay between each closure reduces race conditions with the browser window management layer.           |
| Single-Window No-Op Guard | When only one window handle exists the action returns immediately, making it safe to call in any session state. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case       | Description                                                                                             |
| Popup Cleanup  | Remove advertisement or notification popups spawned during a workflow before continuing processing.     |
| Tab Management | Reduce open tabs to the primary context after a subprocess opens temporary browser windows.             |
| Session Reset  | Enforce a single-window state at the start of each automation iteration without restarting the session. |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case                    | Description                                                                                            |
| Post-Test Window Teardown   | Remove all child windows left open by a test step to prevent state leakage into the next test case.    |
| Partial Window Cleanup      | Close a known subset of popup windows opened during a test flow without closing the main test session. |
| Window State Assertion Prep | Reset to a single-window state before asserting the expected window count in a multi-window flow test. |

edge_cases: Only one window open — no child windows to close
Child window already closed before iteration — failure is captured and process continues
Beforeunload dialog may block the close action
annotations.expected_result: All child browser windows are closed and WebDriver focus returns to the main window.
annotations.notes: Closes all non-main window handles. Failures on individual windows do not stop execution.

description: ### Close all child browser windows
Closes every child window or tab beyond the main window.
Execution continues even if individual window closures fail.

edge_cases: Argument exceeds available child windows — all children are closed
Argument is zero or negative — no windows are closed
Non-numeric argument — treated as default behavior
annotations.expected_result: The specified number of child browser windows are closed and WebDriver focus returns to the main window.
annotations.notes: The Argument limits how many child windows are closed. The value is normalized when out of range.

description: ### Close a specific number of child browser windows
Closes a defined number of child windows based on the provided argument.
Values outside the valid range are normalized automatically.
Argument specifies the maximum number of child browser windows to close.
When omitted or non-numeric, all child windows beyond the main window are closed.
A value greater than the number of available children is silently clamped to that count.
A negative value is normalized to zero, resulting in no closures.
properties.name: Argument

summary: CloseChildWindows closes all child browser windows or a specified number of them, always preserving the main window at index zero and returning driver focus to it after completion.
In robotic process automation it enforces a focused single-window session state by removing unwanted popup or tab windows without disrupting the primary workflow context.
In automation testing it provides clean window teardown between test steps and test cases, preventing residual child windows from leaking state into subsequent validations.
